name: KoliBri
description: KoliBri builds directly on the web standards of the W3C (framework-agnostic), is a generic reference implementation of the WCAG standard and the BITV for accessibility and implemented as a multi-theming capable presentation layer. There is no technical reference and no data transmission functionalities. This means that KoliBri can be reused for the realization of static websites as well as dynamic web applications with different corporate designs and style guides and is therefore very interesting for open source.

components.name: Abbr
components.definition:
The **Abbr** component implements the HTML tag `abbr` with an accessible tooltip.
The tooltip for the description is displayed and read aloud when the **Abbr** component is focused or hovered.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

components.name: Accordion
components.definition:
The **Accordion** component is a collapsible menu. Clicking the header area — consisting of an icon and a heading — expands the content to reveal additional information. It is an interactive navigation element designed to present extensive content in a space-saving manner.

Accordions are used whenever content associated with a thematic heading needs to be shown or hidden. They allow more detailed information for a heading than would normally be practical, leaving it to the users to decide whether to view that information.

concepts.name: disabled
concepts.description: Makes the element not focusable and ignore all events.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: level
concepts.description: Defines which H-level from 1-6 the heading has. 0 specifies no heading and is shown as bold text.

concepts.name: open
concepts.description: Opens/expands the element when truthy, closes/collapses when falsy.

components.name: Alert
components.definition: The **Alert** component provides visual feedback to users. It consists of a color-coded container, a heading, content text, and an icon. The icon used and the color scheme depend on the `_type` of the alert.

concepts.name: alert
concepts.description: Defines whether the screen-readers should read out the notification.

concepts.name: has-closer
concepts.description: Defines whether the element can be closed.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: level
concepts.description: Defines which H-level from 1-6 the heading has. 0 specifies no heading and is shown as bold text.

concepts.name: type
concepts.description: Defines either the type of the component or of the components interactive element.

concepts.name: variant
concepts.description: Defines which variant should be used for presentation.

components.name: Avatar
components.definition: The **Avatar** component displays either a small image of the user or their initials if no image is available.

concepts.name: color
concepts.description: Defines the backgroundColor and foregroundColor.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: src
concepts.description: Sets the image `src` attribute to the given string.

components.name: Badge
components.definition:
The **Badge** component allows you to visually highlight specific information.
In addition to specifying the background color and automatically calculating the text color, it also supports adding an icon and/or a different font style.

concepts.name: color
concepts.description: Defines the backgroundColor and foregroundColor.

concepts.name: icons
concepts.description: Defines the icon classnames (e.g. `_icons="fa-solid fa-user"`).

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: smart-button
concepts.description: Allows to add a button with an arbitrary action within the element (_hide-label only).

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.definition: The **Breadcrumb** component can be used to display the path to the current position of a web page within a hierarchical structure.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: links
concepts.description: Defines the list of links combined with their labels to render.

components.name: Button
components.definition: The **Button** component is used to present users with action options and arrange them in a clear hierarchy. It helps users find the most important actions on a page or within a viewport and allows them to execute those actions. The button label clearly indicates which action will be triggered. Buttons allow users to confirm a change, complete steps in a task, or make decisions.

concepts.name: access-key
concepts.description: Defines the key combination that can be used to trigger or focus the component's interactive element.

concepts.name: aria-controls
concepts.description: Defines which elements are controlled by this component. (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-controls)

concepts.name: aria-description
concepts.description: Defines the value for the aria-description attribute.

concepts.name: aria-expanded
concepts.description: Defines whether the interactive element of the component expanded something. (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-expanded)

concepts.name: aria-selected
concepts.description: Defines whether the interactive element of the component is selected (e.g. role=tab). (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Attributes/aria-selected)

concepts.name: custom-class
concepts.description: Defines the custom class attribute if _variant="custom" is set.

concepts.description: Makes the element not focusable and ignore all events.

Hides the caption by default and displays the caption text with a tooltip when the
interactive element is focused or the mouse is over it.

concepts.description: Defines the icon classnames (e.g. `_icons="fa-solid fa-user"`).

concepts.name: inline
concepts.description: Defines whether the component is displayed as a standalone block or inline without enforcing a minimum size of 44px.

concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.). Set to `false` to enable the expert slot.

concepts.name: short-key
concepts.description: Adds a visual shortcut hint after the label and instructs the screen reader to read the shortcut aloud.

concepts.name: tooltip-align
concepts.description: Defines where to show the Tooltip preferably: top, right, bottom or left.

concepts.description: Defines either the type of the component or of the components interactive element.

concepts.name: value

components.name: ButtonLink
components.definition:
The **ButtonLink** component is semantically a button but has the appearance of a link. All relevant properties of the Button component are adopted and extended with the design-defining properties of a link.

A button can be disabled, therefore the **ButtonLink** also has the `_disabled` property. How this is styled visually is determined by the UX designer.

Instead of using `_href` as with a regular link, the **ButtonLink**'s behavior is controlled via a click callback using the `_on` property.

A link has the `target` property which optionally opens the link in a new window/tab. This behavior is not yet implemented.

Since a link, unlike a button, is not offered in multiple variants (`primary`, `secondary`, etc.), the `_customClass` and `_variant` properties are not available.

components.name: Card
components.definition:
The **Card** component is ideal for visually highlighting individual sections of your website. It allows you to structure your content very easily.

The **Card** component consists of a **_title area_** and a **_content area_**.

The **title area** is displayed in a larger font. The **content area** is visually separated from the title area by a horizontal dividing line and is rendered in the default font.

components.name: Details
components.definition:
The **Details** component allows additional information to be initially shown with a short introductory text,
which is only fully expanded after the user clicks on an arrow icon.

By default, the **Details** component is displayed as a single-line layout element consisting of an arrow icon
followed by a short introductory text. The actual content is revealed below after clicking the header area. The arrow icon
changes its orientation from **_right_** to **_down_**.
The component can also be closed again to hide the content.

components.name: Form
components.definition: The **Form** component is used to wrap all input fields, correctly position the required-fields hint text, and forward the `submit` and `reset` events.

concepts.name: required-text
concepts.description: Defines whether the mandatory-fields-hint should be shown. A string overrides the default text.

components.name: Heading
components.definition: The **Heading** component can be used wherever a heading needs to be displayed. By using the different sizes, content can be clearly structured and pages can be presented effectively and with variety. It separates styling from semantics and enables flexibility.

components.name: Icon
components.definition: The **Icon** component allows icons from included icon fonts to be displayed at any position.

components.name: Image
components.definition: The **Image** component renders an image with support for responsive loading via `srcset` and `sizes`, lazy loading, and accessible alternative text.

components.definition: The **InputRadio** input type consists of a collection of radio elements, providing a choice between different values. Only a single value can be selected at a time. Selected radio elements are typically represented by a filled, visually highlighted circle.

components.name: LinkButton
components.definition: The **LinkButton** component is semantically a link but has the appearance of a button. All relevant properties of the Link component are adopted and extended with the design-defining properties of a button.

components.name: Modal
components.definition: The **Modal** component has been superseded by `kol-dialog`, which provides improved accessibility and conforms to the HTML dialog specification. It is still available in version 2 for backwards compatibility.

concepts.name: label

concepts.description: Defines the width of the modal. (max-width: 100%)

components.name: Nav
components.definition:
The **Nav** component renders a group of related links or navigation elements that perform an action or display content when clicked.
It provides a highly configurable vertical or horizontal navigation bar that can represent multiple levels and vary in width.

concepts.name: collapsible
concepts.description: Defines if navigation nodes can be collapsed or not. Enabled by default.

concepts.name: has-compact-button
concepts.description: Creates a button below the navigation, that toggles _collapsible.

concepts.name: has-icons-when-expanded
concepts.description: Shows icons next to the navigation item labels, even when the navigation is not collapsed.

Hides the caption by default and displays the caption text with a tooltip when the
interactive element is focused or the mouse is over it.

concepts.description: Defines the list of links, buttons or texts to render.

concepts.name: boundary-count
concepts.description: Defines the amount of pages to show next to the outer arrow buttons.

concepts.name: custom-class
concepts.description: Defines the custom class attribute if _variant="custom" is set.

concepts.name: has-buttons
concepts.description: Defines which navigation buttons to render (first, last, next, previous buttons).

concepts.description: Defines the amount of pages to show next to the current page.

components.definition: The **Quote** component has two variants: a short inline (`inline`) and an indented block (`block`) variant. Both variants include a link to the source of the quote.

components.name: SkipNav
components.definition: The **SkipNav** component renders a hidden navigation that allows keyboard and assistive technology users to skip repetitive navigation sections and jump directly to the main content. It only becomes visible when reached via the Tab key.

components.name: Spin
components.definition: The **Spin** component informs users about loading or processing operations being performed by the system. Progress can be communicated through a repeated animation.

components.name: SplitButton
components.definition:
The **SplitButton** component can be used to display a two-part button. The primary button is typically used for
a main action, while the secondary button opens a context menu (`Popover`) that contains additional actions.

concepts.name: tooltip-align
concepts.description: Defines where to show the Tooltip preferably: top, right, bottom or left.

concepts.name: type
concepts.description: Defines either the type of the component or of the components interactive element.

concepts.name: value
concepts.description: Defines the value of the element.

concepts.name: variant
concepts.description: Defines which variant should be used for presentation.

components.name: TableStateful
components.definition: The **Table** component is primarily used for the clear presentation of data sets. It is designed to automatically determine all data-dependent values and render the table accordingly. This includes optional features such as column sorting and pagination.

concepts.name: allow-multi-sort
concepts.description: Defines whether to allow multi sort.

concepts.name: data
concepts.description: Defines the primary table data.

concepts.name: data-foot
concepts.description: Defines the data for the table footer.

concepts.name: has-settings-menu
concepts.description: Enables the settings menu if true (default: false).

concepts.name: headers
concepts.description: Defines the horizontal and vertical table headers.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: pagination
concepts.description: Defines whether to show the data distributed over multiple pages.

concepts.name: pagination-position
concepts.description: Controls the position of the pagination.

concepts.name: selection
concepts.description: Defines how rows can be selected and the current selection.

concepts.name: data
concepts.description: Defines the primary table data.

concepts.name: data-foot
concepts.description: Defines the data for the table footer.

concepts.name: has-settings-menu
concepts.description: Enables the settings menu if true (default: false).

concepts.name: header-cells
concepts.description: Defines the horizontal and vertical table headers.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: selection
concepts.description: Defines how rows can be selected and the current selection.

components.name: Tabs
components.definition: The **Tabs** component is used to organize related content on the same page and navigate between them. Tabs ensure that large amounts of content can be more easily organized for users.

concepts.name: align
concepts.description: Defines the visual orientation of the component.

concepts.name: behavior
concepts.description: Defines which behavior is active.

concepts.name: has-create-button
concepts.description: Defines whether the element has a create button.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: selected
concepts.description: Defines which tab is active.

concepts.name: tabs
concepts.description: Defines the tab captions.

components.name: Textarea
components.definition: The **Textarea** component provides a larger input field for content. Unlike InputText, it also allows extensive content to be entered, including line breaks.

concepts.name: access-key
concepts.description: Defines the key combination that can be used to trigger or focus the component's interactive element.

concepts.name: adjust-height
concepts.description: Adjusts the height of the element to its content.

concepts.name: disabled
concepts.description: Makes the element not focusable and ignore all events.

concepts.name: has-counter
concepts.description: Shows a character counter for the input element.

concepts.name: hide-label
concepts.description:
Hides the caption by default and displays the caption text with a tooltip when the
interactive element is focused or the mouse is over it.

concepts.name: hide-msg
concepts.description: Hides the error message but leaves it in the DOM for the input's aria-describedby.

concepts.name: hint
concepts.description: Defines the hint text.

concepts.name: icons
concepts.description: Defines the icon classnames (e.g. `_icons="fa-solid fa-user"`).

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.). Set to `false` to enable the expert slot.

concepts.name: max-length
concepts.description: Defines the maximum number of input characters.

concepts.name: max-length-behavior
concepts.description: Defines the behavior when maxLength is set. 'hard' sets the maxlength attribute, 'soft' shows a character counter without preventing input.

concepts.name: msg
concepts.description: Defines the properties for a message rendered as Alert component.

concepts.name: name
concepts.description: Defines the technical name of an input field.

concepts.name: placeholder
concepts.description: Defines the placeholder for input field. To be shown when there's no value.

concepts.name: read-only
concepts.description: Makes the input element read only.

concepts.name: required
concepts.description: Makes the input element required.

concepts.name: resize
concepts.description:
Defines whether and in which direction the size of the input can be changed by the user. (https://developer.mozilla.org/de/docs/Web/CSS/resize)
In version 3 (v3), horizontal resizing is abolished. The corresponding property is then reduced to the properties `vertical` (default) and `none`.

concepts.name: rows
concepts.description: Maximum number of visible rows of the element.

concepts.name: short-key
concepts.description: Adds a visual shortcut hint after the label and instructs the screen reader to read the shortcut aloud.

concepts.name: spell-check
concepts.description: Defines whether the browser should check the spelling and grammar.

concepts.name: tooltip-align
concepts.description: Defines where to show the Tooltip preferably: top, right, bottom or left.

concepts.name: touched
concepts.description: Shows if the input was touched by a user.

concepts.name: value
concepts.description: Defines the value of the element.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).

concepts.name: orientation
concepts.description: Defines whether the orientation of the component is horizontal or vertical.

concepts.name: label
concepts.description: Defines the visible or semantic label of the component (e.g. aria-label, label, headline, caption, summary, etc.).
